name: DynamoDB On-Demand to Provisioned Capacity
description: Switch DynamoDB table from on-demand to provisioned capacity mode for cost optimization

template.description: Converting DynamoDB table '{{tableName}}' from on-demand billing to provisioned capacity mode. Current average usage: {{avgReadCapacity}} RCU and {{avgWriteCapacity}} WCU. Peak traffic is {{peakMultiplier}}x average. Will configure auto-scaling to handle traffic spikes while optimizing costs.
template.currentState: DynamoDB table '{{tableName}}' using on-demand billing mode. Handles variable traffic with automatic scaling. Average throughput: {{avgReadCapacity}} reads/sec and {{avgWriteCapacity}} writes/sec. No throttling observed.
template.proposedState: Provisioned capacity mode with base capacity of {{avgReadCapacity}} RCU and {{avgWriteCapacity}} WCU. Auto-scaling enabled with target utilization 70%, min capacity 50% of base, max capacity {{peakMultiplier}}x base. CloudWatch alarms for throttling.
template.trafficPatterns: Predictable daily pattern with peak hours 10 AM - 4 PM. Weekend traffic is 40% of weekday. Occasional spikes during marketing campaigns ({{peakMultiplier}}x normal). Read-heavy workload with 80/20 read-write ratio.
